steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Identify the right angle and triangle structure
steps.content: Since angles $A$ and $B$ are acute in right triangle $ABC$, the right angle must be at $C$. This makes $AC$ and $BC$ the two legs of the triangle.

steps.title: Set up the tangent ratio for angle B
steps.content:
For angle $B$ in a right triangle:
$$\tan B = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{AC}{BC}$$
Substituting the known values $\tan B = \frac{1}{7}$ and $AC = 23.2$:
$$\frac{1}{7} = \frac{23.2}{BC}$$
